summary: Iteration 2: Full E2E re-test of flipped-role HomeNest (sitter sends form → client fills). Backend: rewrote backend_test.py for new FormCreateIn={title,client_name,client_email}, new POST /api/public/forms/{share_token}/submit, status lifecycle, 503 email path, auth guards — 26/26 PASS. Frontend: landing copy, auth guard, register→dashboard, NewForm create (pending), SitterFormView pending banner + share link + copy + prefilled client email + send-email 503 toast, PublicForm intro + 6-step wizard (calendar date picking, stay, pets, feeding+walks, tasks, contacts) + submit, dashboard Completed group, sitter completed banner/print, public revisit read-only CarePlanView — all working.

minor.issue: Not auth-protected; always returns 200. Same as iteration 1 — unchanged.

client_email has no EmailStr validation (accepts any free-form string, then .lower() on it). Minor — invalid emails can be stored (e.g. 'notemail'). Consider EmailStr with Optional or a pydantic validator.

ui_bugs.issue: Thank-you view (data-testid=thank-you-view) never renders after submit because parent PublicForm rechecks form.status after onSubmitted() → instantly swaps to the completed CarePlanView. Spec says Submit → thank-you view. Functionally fine (read-only plan is shown) but the celebratory screen is skipped.

action_items: (Optional/low) Fix PublicForm so the in-wizard 'Thank you!' view is shown after submit (e.g. branch on a local submitted state before the form.status completed check, or render the thank-you inline when onSubmitted is triggered). Currently user sees CarePlanView immediately.
(Optional) Add EmailStr validation or pydantic validator for FormCreateIn.client_email and FormMetaUpdateIn.client_email to prevent malformed emails from being persisted.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 412: public_form projection correctly excludes user_id and sitter_email — matches spec.
server.py line 418-436: submit_public_form correctly rejects already-completed forms with 400 and strips user_id/sitter_email from returned payload.
server.py line 349-369: create_form stores empty submission defaults — clean shape. Share token uses secrets.token_urlsafe(16) → ~22 char URL-safe token, good.
PublicForm.jsx lines 40-62: form.status===completed branch returns CarePlanView before FillableFlow — causes thank-you-view bypass (see ui_bugs).
Admin seed (lines 534-549) correctly re-hashes admin password if changed in env — good idempotent behaviour.
CORS allow_origins explicit (FRONTEND_URL + localhost) with credentials — correct; cookies use samesite=none + secure=true for cross-origin preview domain.
JWT secret read from env; bcrypt $2b$ hash format verified via successful login cycle.
FormSteps.jsx is 670+ lines — suggest splitting per-step components into separate files once further steps/logic are added (maintainability).
SitterFormView.jsx send-email form: uses RESEND_API_KEY 503 detail verbatim as toast — good, tested visually.
test_credentials: Seeded admin admin@housesit.app/admin123 verified. New sitters registered dynamically with TEST_sitter_{uuid}@example.com / TestPass123!.
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_-prefixed users/forms created for this run and left in DB (each run uses a unique uuid suffix so no collision).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py fully covers new flipped schema (26 tests). FormCreateIn={title, client_name, client_email}; submit via POST /api/public/forms/{share_token}/submit (no auth). RESEND_API_KEY intentionally empty → send-email returns 503 (verified twice). Google OAuth requires real Emergent session_id (expect 401/502 for fakes). Step 1 of public wizard requires at least one date selected in DayPicker calendar (Next is disabled until fill.date_start is set). Step 3 requires pet names when pets present.
rca_of_the_issue: Thank-you-view bypass RCA: In PublicForm.jsx, the parent component branches on `form.status === 'completed'` BEFORE rendering FillableFlow. In FillableFlow.submit(), onSubmitted(updatedForm) is called which propagates the newly-completed form back up, causing an immediate re-render that replaces FillableFlow with the read-only CarePlanView. The local `submitted` state inside FillableFlow never gets a chance to render the thank-you view. Mitigation: either (a) keep the thank-you view at the parent level gated by a separate `justSubmitted` state, or (b) delay calling onSubmitted until after a brief timeout (not recommended), or (c) render the CarePlanView branch only when !justSubmitted. Best fix is (a).
